title: Grad/Prof Relaxation Station
description:
In celebration of Graduate and Professional Student Appreciation Week, you are cordially invited to the Relaxation Station sponsored by the Ohio Union Activities Board!

Please join us for an afternoon of massages, crafts, and games in celebration and appreciation for you and your friends!

No tickets or preregistration is required, just a valid BUCKID. Entry is first come, first serve.

title: 64th Annual Mack Jr. Lecture Series
description:
The Mack Lecture has a long-standing and distinguished tradition at OSU, established in honor of Professor Edward Mack, Jr., who was deeply committed to supporting graduate students both academically and personally. This year’s Mack Lecture will feature Prof. Shannon S. Stahl (University of Wisconsin-Madison), presenting “Benzylic C–H Functionalization and Cross-Coupling,” .

These help us customize your site experience using your previous behavior and choices as our guide.

title: Wellness Wednesday: Your Spiritual Compass: Finding Meaning, Purpose, and Peace in Graduate/Professional Student Life
description:
Graduate and professional school can be an intense journey, often leaving little time for reflection on what truly gives life meaning. Join us for an interactive session where we explore the dimension of Spiritual Wellness—a source of strength rooted in your personal values, beliefs, and connection to purpose. We will demystify what spiritual wellness is (hint: it's not just about religion!), share why it's a critical factor for student mental/emotional health and resilience, and provide tangible practices and resources to help you cultivate peace, manage stress, and thrive during your time at OSU and beyond.

Presented by:Adrienne Wilson and Janele Bayless

These help us customize your site experience using your previous behavior and choices as our guide.

title: OUAB Quiz Night
description:
Join us as grad/prof students get together for a night of trivia and fun! Pre-arranged and impromptu-created teams compete in a night of trivia. Prizes are awarded to the top team of the evening. Check in begins at 5:30 PM. First question begins at 6:00 PM.

All teams must register for each quiz night. Teams may include up to 50% non-student guests. Due to overwhelming popularity, registrations the day of the event may not be available. Check out the website for fuller details. The registration period for a quiz night begins at the conclusion of the previous quiz night.

title: Dining Hall Crawl
description:
Time: Friday, April 10, 2026 5:00 PM - 7:00 PM

Graduating students! Have you ever wanted to relive your underclassmen days of eating in the dining hall? This is your chance. OUAB is hosting the first ever, Dining Hall Crawl on April 10th from 5-7pm.

This event is first come, first serve. Please keep an eye out @ouab for details.

Note: This event is weather permitting, meaning that if inclement weather occurs they event will be cancelled. Thank you for understanding.

This event is for SPRING 2026 seniors

title: Beyond the Menu: An Evening of Conversation and Ideas
description:
Join us for Beyond the Menu: An Evening of Conversation and Ideas! The event will feature a speaker-panel focused on accessibility, a service project open-house, and delicious food. It's a great chance to learn more about issues in accessibility and build new connections while playing fun games and entering our fabulous raffle. RSVP at go.osu.edu/beyondthemenu.

title: Graduate Teaching Toolkit: Teaching as a Graduate Student
description:
Many graduate students will confront the challenges associated with course design: either as a graduate student instructor, while preparing materials for the academic job market, or in their early career post-graduation. This semester, in Graduate Teaching Toolkit, the Drake Institute’s workshop series for graduate instructors, we’ll explore each step of the course design process. Toolkit workshops are designed to support participants’ professional development, engage them in reflecting on, discussing, and sharing their teaching practices, and build community between graduate instructors on campus who are passionate about improving their teaching. All graduate students, regardless of teaching experience, are welcome to attend.

Presented by: The Drake Institute for Teaching and Learning

These help us customize your site experience using your previous behavior and choices as our guide.

title: AFNR Spring into Wellness Paint & Pause
description:
The AFNR Mindfulness & Resiliency Workshop is a 120-minute structured session designed specifically for students at any level. Students from other departments are also welcome in this activity. The event will be divided into two sessions: 1) Peer Support Circle: A facilitated "Check-in" where students can discuss the specific stressors of the academic journey in a safe and collaborative space. 2) Expert-Led Guided Practice: PhD students from the College of Nursing who are working on research related to mindfulness activities will use painting as a mindfulness tool, tailored for high-stress academic environments.

title: Flicks For Free: Wicked: For Good
description:
Join OUAB for a movie screening of Wicked: For Good.

Now demonized as the Wicked Witch of the West, Elphaba lives in exile in the Ozian forest, while Glinda resides at the palace in Emerald City, reveling in the perks of fame and popularity. As an angry mob rises against the Wicked Witch, she'll need to reunite with Glinda to transform herself, and all of Oz, for good.

title: OUAB in the Kitchen
description:
Join OUAB in the Instructional Kitchen at the Ohio Union for a chance to cook a meal of your own under the guidance of the Union's own professional chef, Chef Flores. Following a cooking demonstration by the chef, students will collaborate with class members to create the menu providing a unique hands-on learning opportunity. In this fun and interactive environment students will enjoy a delicious meal and gain useful culinary skills to show off in their own kitchens. Whether you're are a master chef in your own kitchen or a novice, there is an OUAB in the Kitchen program for you.

title: 2026 Fashion Production Association Runway Show
description:
Our show this year will be held at the Archie Griffin Ballroom at The Ohio Union, on Saturday, April 18th. Doors open at 6:00 PM, when our Silent Auction will be held to benefit our designers this year, who have purchased their own materials for their collections. The show officially starts at 7:00 PM.

title: Wellness Wednesday: The Mandalas of Transition: Creative Grounding for Navigating your Next Chapter
description:
Join us for our final Wellness Wednesday session where we will be focusing on engaging creative wellness as a powerful tool for stress relief and emotional processing. During this interactive session we will be creating journey mandalas which can serve as a visual map reflecting your hard work, efforts, and pathways forward towards your next chapter (no artistic experience required!). Come take a break with us and finish your semester grounded and strong!

title: Spring Candlelight Ceremony
description:
We are excited to invite all graduating students, their families, and guests to the Commencement Eve Candlelight Ceremony. This special event is a time to reflect, celebrate, and kick off the commencement festivities with a memorable experience.

Graduates and their families should arrive at the Archie Griffin Grand Ballroom (Ohio Union Second Floor) to check in and receive their candles for the ceremony.

Graduates will also be able to pick up a small token of appreciation.

Light refreshments and music will be available for everyone to enjoy while waiting for the event to begin.

Ceremony Start:The ceremony will begin promptly at 9:00 PM, and we kindly ask everyone to be ready as the event will start on time.

We look forward to sharing this special evening with all of you as we celebrate the accomplishments of our graduates. See you there!

These help us customize your site experience using your previous behavior and choices as our guide.
location: Main Oval, Rain Site: Archie Griffin Grand Ball Room
